summary: Full stack testing of VibeForge Studios website. All 14 backend API tests passed. Frontend: all 11 routes verified, waitlist modal works end-to-end, duplicate detection works, product pages load correctly with proper themes, footer attribution and Habit legal links verified.

minor.issue: Rate limiter is per-IP (5 req/hour). During testing from same IP (backend tests + UI tests), rate limit was hit (429). This is expected behavior but very low limit for legitimate users.

integration_issues.issue: Support form showed '429 Too many requests' during UI testing due to rate limit already hit by backend tests on same IP. Form fields and UI work correctly.

issues: data-theme='studioapp' instead of 'studio' - minor naming inconsistency, but theme does apply

critical_code_review_comments: Support rate limit is 5 req/hour per IP - may be too restrictive for legitimate users who retry
Console warning: '<span> cannot be child of <option>' hydration warning from WaitlistModal PRODUCTS map - <span> wrapper around <option> elements. Not a breaking bug but should be fixed.
Studio page data-theme='studioapp' vs 'studio' - verify this is intentional for CSS theming

success_rate.frontend: 95% (all pages load, all core flows work)

seed_data_creation: TEST_ prefixed waitlist entries: TEST_eco@vibetest.com, TEST_habit@vibetest.com, TEST_studio@vibetest.com, TEST_desk@vibetest.com, TEST_support@vibetest.com (support). playwright_test_unique99@vibetest.com in waitlist.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All core flows verified. Backend 100% passing. Key findings: (1) WaitlistModal has a React console warning about <span> inside <option> - low severity. (2) Studio page data-theme is 'studioapp' not 'studio'. (3) Support rate limit 5/hr may block legitimate users. Test credentials: none needed (no auth). Rate limit on /api/support was hit during testing so UI form showed 429.
